verdict_reasoning: TMED10 (TMP21/p23) is a single-pass type I transmembrane protein belonging to the p24/EMP24 family. Its dominant home is the ER-Golgi and ERGIC compartment, where it functions as a COPI/COPII cargo receptor. However, multiple independent lines of evidence document a genuine plasma-membrane pool: (1) NCBI/RefSeq explicitly states it is 'localized to the plasma membrane and Golgi cisternae'; (2) Blum & Lepier (2008) showed the luminal domain mediates cell-surface trafficking; (3) TMED10 has been shown to cycle through the plasma membrane with GPI-anchored proteins; (4) its cytoplasmic tail drives ER retention but the lumenal domain partially overrides this; (5) RAB21 modulates TMED10 recycling from the PM back to the Golgi. The PM pool is real but minority relative to the dominant Golgi/ER compartment, making this dual_localization / contextual rather than a stable constitutive surface receptor. The ectodomain (GOLD domain) faces the extracellular/lumenal side, so when at the PM it is outer-leaflet accessible.
